{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tokkehara Kofun / Tonkehara Kofun |image = [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and Stone Chamber Opening |別名 = |part_of = |所在地 = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Higashiibaraki District|ja|東茨城郡}}, {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}} Kitakata 1478-1, others (Tokke area)
(within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 13.03 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 27.05 |ISO = JP-08 |形状 = Rectangular mound |規模 = 37.8m×24m |埋葬施設 = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |出土品 = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |陪塚 = |築造時期 = Late 7th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=30|lat_sec=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. The mound is a rectangular burial mound and is designated as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single burial mound on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the former Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue ware have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official uniform belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The site was designated a historic site by the former Katsura Village in 1973, and this designation is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following dimensions: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Cultural Properties == === Shirosato Town Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (designated January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue ware have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official uniform belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The site was designated a historic site by the former Katsura Village in 1973, and this designation is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following dimensions: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Cultural Properties == === Shirosato Town Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (designated January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tokkehara Kofun / Tonkehara Kofun |image = [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and Stone Chamber Opening |別名 = |part_of = |所在地 = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Higashiibaraki District|ja|東茨城郡}}, {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}} Kitakata 1478-1, others (Tokke area)
(within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 13.03 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 27.05 |ISO = JP-08 |形状 = Rectangular mound |規模 = 37.8m×24m |埋葬施設 = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |出土品 = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |陪塚 = |築造時期 = Late 7th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=30|lat_sec=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. The mound is a rectangular burial mound and is designated as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single burial mound on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the former Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue ware have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official uniform belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The site was designated a historic site by the former Katsura Village in 1973, and this designation is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following dimensions: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Cultural Properties == === Shirosato Town Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (designated January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tokkehara Kofun / Tonkehara Kofun |image = [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and Stone Chamber Opening |別名 = |part_of = |所在地 = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Higashiibaraki District|ja|東茨城郡}}, {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}} Kitakata 1478-1, others (Tokke area)
(within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 13.03 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 27.05 |ISO = JP-08 |形状 = Rectangular mound |規模 = 37.8m×24m |埋葬施設 = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |出土品 = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |陪塚 = |築造時期 = Late 7th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=30|lat_sec=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. The mound is a rectangular burial mound and is designated as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single burial mound on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the former Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue ware have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official uniform belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The site was designated a historic site by the former Katsura Village in 1973, and this designation is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following dimensions: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Cultural Properties == === Shirosato Town Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (designated January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] (within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 13.03 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 27.05 |ISO = JP-08 |形状 = Rectangular mound |規模 = 37.8m×24m |埋葬施設 = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |出土品 = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |陪塚 = |築造時期 = Late 7th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=30|lat_sec=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. The mound is a rectangular burial mound and is designated as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single burial mound on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the former Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue ware have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official uniform belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The site was designated a historic site by the former Katsura Village in 1973, and this designation is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following dimensions: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Cultural Properties == === Shirosato Town Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (designated January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tokkehara Kofun / Tonkehara Kofun |image = [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and Stone Chamber Opening |別名 = |part_of = |所在地 = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Higashiibaraki District|ja|東茨城郡}}, {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}} Kitakata 1478-1, others (Tokke area)
(within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 13.03 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 27.05 |ISO = JP-08 |形状 = Rectangular mound |規模 = 37.8m×24m |埋葬施設 = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |出土品 = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |陪塚 = |築造時期 = Late 7th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=30|lat_sec=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. The mound is a rectangular burial mound and is designated as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single burial mound on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the former Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue ware have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official uniform belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The site was designated a historic site by the former Katsura Village in 1973, and this designation is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following dimensions: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Cultural Properties == === Shirosato Town Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (designated January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tokkehara Kofun / Tonkehara Kofun |image = [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and Stone Chamber Opening |別名 = |part_of = |所在地 = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Higashiibaraki District|ja|東茨城郡}}, {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}} Kitakata 1478-1, others (Tokke area)
(within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 13.03 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 27.05 |ISO = JP-08 |形状 = Rectangular mound |規模 = 37.8m×24m |埋葬施設 = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |出土品 = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |陪塚 = |築造時期 = Late 7th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=30|lat_sec=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. The mound is a rectangular burial mound and is designated as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single burial mound on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the former Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue ware have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official uniform belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The site was designated a historic site by the former Katsura Village in 1973, and this designation is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following dimensions: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Cultural Properties == === Shirosato Town Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (designated January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone The mound is a rectangular burial mound and is designated as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single burial mound on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the former Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue ware have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official uniform belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The site was designated a historic site by the former Katsura Village in 1973, and this designation is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following dimensions: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Cultural Properties == === Shirosato Town Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (designated January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tokkehara Kofun / Tonkehara Kofun |image = [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and Stone Chamber Opening |別名 = |part_of = |所在地 = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Higashiibaraki District|ja|東茨城郡}}, {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}} Kitakata 1478-1, others (Tokke area)
(within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 13.03 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 27.05 |ISO = JP-08 |形状 = Rectangular mound |規模 = 37.8m×24m |埋葬施設 = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室}} |出土品 = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |陪塚 = |築造時期 = Late 7th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=30|lat_sec=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. The mound is a rectangular burial mound and is designated as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single burial mound on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the former Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue ware have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official uniform belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The site was designated a historic site by the former Katsura Village in 1973, and this designation is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following dimensions: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Cultural Properties == === Shirosato Town Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (designated January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Tokkehara Kofun / Tonkehara Kofun |image = [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and Stone Chamber Opening |別名 = |part_of = |所在地 = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Higashiibaraki District|ja|東茨城郡}}, {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}} Kitakata 1478-1, others (Tokke area)
(within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 13.03 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 27.05 |ISO = JP-08 |形状 = Rectangular mound |規模 = 37.8m×24m |埋葬施設 = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |出土品 = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |陪塚 = |築造時期 = Late 7th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=30|lat_sec=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. The mound is a rectangular burial mound and is designated as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single burial mound on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the former Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue ware have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official uniform belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The site was designated a historic site by the former Katsura Village in 1973, and this designation is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following dimensions: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Cultural Properties == === Shirosato Town Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (designated January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name = Tokkehara Kofun / Tonkehara Kofun |image = [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and Stone Chamber Opening |別名 = |part_of = |所在地 = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Higashiibaraki District|ja|東茨城郡}}, {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}} Kitakata 1478-1, others (Tokke area)
(within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 13.03 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 27.05 |ISO = JP-08 |形状 = Rectangular mound |規模 = 37.8m×24m |埋葬施設 = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |出土品 = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |陪塚 = |築造時期 = Late 7th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=30|lat_sec=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. The mound is a rectangular burial mound and is designated as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single burial mound on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the former Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue ware have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official uniform belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The site was designated a historic site by the former Katsura Village in 1973, and this designation is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following dimensions: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Cultural Properties == === Shirosato Town Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (designated January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] Mound and Stone Chamber Opening |別名 = |part_of = |所在地 = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Higashiibaraki District|ja|東茨城郡}}, {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}} Kitakata 1478-1, others (Tokke area)
(within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 13.03 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 27.05 |ISO = JP-08 |形状 = Rectangular mound |規模 = 37.8m×24m |埋葬施設 = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |出土品 = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |陪塚 = |築造時期 = Late 7th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=30|lat_sec=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. The mound is a rectangular burial mound and is designated as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single burial mound on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the former Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue ware have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official uniform belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The site was designated a historic site by the former Katsura Village in 1973, and this designation is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following dimensions: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Cultural Properties == === Shirosato Town Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (designated January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] The mound is a rectangular burial mound and is designated as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single burial mound on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the former Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue ware have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official uniform belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The site was designated a historic site by the former Katsura Village in 1973, and this designation is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following dimensions: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Cultural Properties == === Shirosato Town Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (designated January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] (within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 13.03 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 27.05 |ISO = JP-08 |形状 = Rectangular mound |規模 = 37.8m×24m |埋葬施設 = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |出土品 = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |陪塚 = |築造時期 = Late 7th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=30|lat_sec=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. The mound is a rectangular burial mound and is designated as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single burial mound on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the former Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue ware have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official uniform belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The site was designated a historic site by the former Katsura Village in 1973, and this designation is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following dimensions: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Cultural Properties == === Shirosato Town Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (designated January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] Mound and Stone Chamber Opening |別名 = |part_of = |所在地 = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}, {{ill|Higashiibaraki District|ja|東茨城郡}}, {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}} Kitakata 1478-1, others (Tokke area)
(within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 13.03 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 27.05 |ISO = JP-08 |形状 = Rectangular mound |規模 = 37.8m×24m |埋葬施設 = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber}} |出土品 = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}}, {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} |陪塚 = |築造時期 = Late 7th century |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=30|lat_sec=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県}}. The mound is a rectangular burial mound and is designated as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single burial mound on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the former Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where artifacts such as {{ill|Sue ware|ja|須恵器}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji ware|ja|土師器}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue ware have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official uniform belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制}}. The site was designated a historic site by the former Katsura Village in 1973, and this designation is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == Burial Facility == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following dimensions: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Cultural Properties == === Shirosato Town Designated Cultural Properties === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (designated January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried Cultural Property Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education]
