The mound is a rectangular Kofun and is Historic Site1 as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川|12=draft}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single Kofun on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the typeer Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where burial goods such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue pottery have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official unitype belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer Katsura Village in 1973, and this Historic Site is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災|12=draft}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == burial == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following area: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Historic Site == === Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (Historic Site1 January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shirosato]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofuns]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tokkehara Kofun]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]] [[ja:徳化原古墳]] [[sn:oku Kahara Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] {{Kofun navbar}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|en|12=draft}} |name = Tokkehara Kofun / Tonkehara Kofun |image = [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and Stone Chamber Opening |別名 = | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|12=draft}}, {{ill|Higashiibaraki District|ja|東茨城郡|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町|12=draft}} Kitakata 1478-1, others (Tokke area)
(within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center) |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 13.03 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 27.05 |omitted = JP-08 |type = Rectangular mound |area = 37.8m×24m |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft}} |burial goods = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} |陪塚 = |築造時期 = Late 7th century |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|coordinates=36|coordinates=30|coordinates=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|map_dot_label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町|12=draft}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|12=draft}}. The mound is a rectangular Kofun and is Historic Site1 as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川|12=draft}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single Kofun on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the typeer Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where burial goods such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue pottery have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official unitype belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer Katsura Village in 1973, and this Historic Site is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災|12=draft}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == burial == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following area: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Historic Site == === Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (Historic Site1 January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]]{{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shirosato]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofuns]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tokkehara Kofun]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]] [[ja:徳化原古墳]] [[sn:oku Kahara Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]][[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]][[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]]{{Kofun navbar}}

The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer Katsura Village in 1973, and this Historic Site is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災|12=draft|12=draft}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == burial == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following area: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Historic Site == === Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (Historic Site1 January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shirosato]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofuns]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tokkehara Kofun]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]] [[ja:徳化原古墳]] [[sn:oku Kahara Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|en|12=draft|12=draft}} |name = Tokkehara Kofun / Tonkehara Kofun |image = [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and Stone Chamber Opening |alternate_name = | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Higashiibaraki District|ja|東茨城郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町|12=draft|12=draft}} Kitakata 1478-1, others (Tokke area)
(within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center) |coordinates = 36|coordinates = 30|coordinates = 13.03 |coordinates =140|coordinates = 21|coordinates = 27.05 |omitted = JP-08 |type = Rectangular mound |area = 37.8m×24m |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} |陪塚 = |built = Late 7th century |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|coordinates=36|coordinates=30|coordinates=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|map_dot_label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound is a rectangular Kofun and is Historic Site1 as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single Kofun on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the typeer Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where burial goods such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue pottery have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft|12=draft}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official unitype belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer Katsura Village in 1973, and this Historic Site is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災|12=draft|12=draft}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == burial == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following area: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Historic Site == === Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (Historic Site1 January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shirosato]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofuns]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tokkehara Kofun]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]] [[ja:徳化原古墳]] [[sn:oku Kahara Kofun]]

The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official unitype belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer Katsura Village in 1973, and this Historic Site is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災|12=draft|12=draft}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == burial == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following area: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Historic Site == === Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (Historic Site1 January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shirosato]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofuns]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tokkehara Kofun]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]] [[ja:徳化原古墳]] [[sn:oku Kahara Kofun]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|en|12=draft|12=draft}} |name = Tokkehara Kofun / Tonkehara Kofun |image = [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and Stone Chamber Opening |alternate_name = | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Higashiibaraki District|ja|東茨城郡|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町|12=draft|12=draft}} Kitakata 1478-1, others (Tokke area)
(within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center) |coordinates = 36|coordinates = 30|coordinates = 13.03 |coordinates =140|coordinates = 21|coordinates = 27.05 |omitted = JP-08 |type = Rectangular mound |area = 37.8m×24m |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} |陪塚 = |built = Late 7th century |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|coordinates=36|coordinates=30|coordinates=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|map_dot_label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound is a rectangular Kofun and is Historic Site1 as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single Kofun on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the typeer Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where burial goods such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue pottery have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft|12=draft}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official unitype belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer Katsura Village in 1973, and this Historic Site is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災|12=draft|12=draft}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == burial == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following area: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Historic Site == === Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (Historic Site1 January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shirosato]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofuns]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tokkehara Kofun]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]] [[ja:徳化原古墳]] [[sn:oku Kahara Kofun]]

The mound is surrounded by a moat, where burial goods such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue pottery have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft|12=draft}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official unitype belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer Katsura Village in 1973, and this Historic Site is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災|12=draft|12=draft}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == burial == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following area: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Historic Site == === Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (Historic Site1 January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shirosato]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofuns]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tokkehara Kofun]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]] [[ja:徳化原古墳]] [[sn:oku Kahara Kofun]]

(within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center) |coordinates = 36|coordinates = 30|coordinates = 13.03 |coordinates =140|coordinates = 21|coordinates = 27.05 |omitted = JP-08 |type = Rectangular mound |area = 37.8m×24m |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} |陪塚 = |built = Late 7th century |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|coordinates=36|coordinates=30|coordinates=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|map_dot_label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound is a rectangular Kofun and is Historic Site1 as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single Kofun on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the typeer Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where burial goods such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue pottery have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft|12=draft}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official unitype belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer Katsura Village in 1973, and this Historic Site is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災|12=draft|12=draft}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == burial == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following area: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Historic Site == === Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (Historic Site1 January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shirosato]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofuns]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tokkehara Kofun]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]] [[ja:徳化原古墳]] [[sn:oku Kahara Kofun]]

(within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center) |coordinates = 36|coordinates = 30|coordinates = 13.03 |coordinates =140|coordinates = 21|coordinates = 27.05 |omitted = JP-08 |type = Rectangular mound |area = 37.8m×24m |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=draft|12=draft}} |burial goods = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} |陪塚 = |built = Late 7th century |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|coordinates=36|coordinates=30|coordinates=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|map_dot_label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町|12=draft|12=draft}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|12=draft|12=draft}}. The mound is a rectangular Kofun and is Historic Site1 as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川|12=draft|12=draft}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single Kofun on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the typeer Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|12=draft|12=draft}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|12=draft|12=draft}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where burial goods such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|12=draft|12=draft}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue pottery have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=draft|12=draft}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official unitype belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|12=draft|12=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer Katsura Village in 1973, and this Historic Site is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災|12=draft|12=draft}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == burial == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following area: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Historic Site == === Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (Historic Site1 January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shirosato]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofuns]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tokkehara Kofun]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]] [[ja:徳化原古墳]] [[sn:oku Kahara Kofun]]

revision.comment: 5 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:徳化原古墳 up to rev 99995243
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 徳化原古墳 / 頓化原古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[茨城県]][[東茨城郡]][[城里町]]北方1478-1ほか（字徳化）
（茨城県埋蔵文化財センター内） |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 13.03 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 27.05 |ISO = JP-08 |形状 = 長方形墳 |規模 = 37.8m×24m |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 青銅製帯金具・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 城里町指定史跡「頓（徳）化原古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2= {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=30|lat_sec=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|label=徳化原古墳|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=茨城県内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''徳化原古墳'''（とっけはらこふん）または'''頓化原古墳'''は、[[茨城県]][[東茨城郡]][[城里町]]北方（きたかた）にある[[古墳]]。形状は長方形墳。城里町指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「頓（徳）化原古墳」）。 == 概要 == 茨城県北部、[[那珂川]]南岸の台地上斜面において山寄せで築造された単独古墳である。現在は旧北方小学校校舎（現在は茨城県埋蔵文化財センター「いせきぴあ茨城」）北側に所在する。[[江戸時代]]以前から石室が開口したといい、近年に測量・発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は長方形で、東西37.8メートル・南北24（22-30）メートルを測る{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}（調査前は[[前方後円墳]]とする説もあった）。墳丘は多段築（4段以上）の階段ピラミッド構造で、墳丘上には柱穴列が認められる{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。また墳丘周囲には周溝が巡らされ、周溝隅部を中心に[[須恵器]]（壺）・[[土師器]]（甕・高坏・坏）が検出されている{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。凝灰質泥岩の切石によって構築された整美な石室で、玄室・羨道からなる単室構造である{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。石室内の副葬品は失われているが、調査では青銅製帯金具（鉸具）・須恵器片が検出されている{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。 築造時期は[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。特に青銅製帯金具は官僚制における初期唐風官服の帯の一部とされ、[[律令制]]施行に際して地方における地域再編の実態を知るうえで重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。 古墳域は[[1973年]]（[[昭和]]48年）に旧桂村指定史跡に指定されている（現在は城里町指定史跡）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1973年]]（[[昭和]]48年）1月20日、旧桂村指定史跡（現在は城里町指定史跡）に指定。 * [[1984年]]（昭和59年）、村史編纂に伴う発掘調査。その後に石室補修（桂村教育委員会、[[1985年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。 * [[2011年]]（[[平成]]23年）、[[東日本大震災]]に伴う石室損壊。その後に石室補修{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。 * [[2015年]]（平成27年）、茨城県埋蔵文化財センター「いせきぴあ茨城」の開設{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。 * [[2016年]]（平成28年）、測量調査（[[茨城大学]]考古学研究室、[[2018年]]に報告）{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。 * [[2018年|2018]]-[[2019年]]度（平成30-[[令和]]元年度）、範囲確認調査：第1・2次調査（茨城大学考古学研究室・城里町教育委員会、[[2021年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室 玄室}}]] 埋葬施設としては[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。玄室・羨道からなる単室構造の石室である。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。 * 石室全長：現存3.58メートル * 玄室：長さ2.30メートル、幅1.70メートル、高さ1.75メートル * 羨道：長さ1.28メートル（玄門含む）、幅1.69メートル 石室の石材は凝灰質泥岩の切石で、羨道は礫床である。 == 文化財 == === 城里町指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 頓（徳）化原古墳 - 1973年（昭和48年）1月20日指定[https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html 頓（徳）化原古墳]（城里町教育委員会）。。 == 関連施設 == * 茨城県埋蔵文化財センター「いせきぴあ茨城」（城里町北方） - 徳化原古墳に隣接。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（城里町教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=徳化古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 8 茨城県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490085|ref={{Harvid|徳化古墳群（平凡社）|1982}}}} * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html 「頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 -茨城県城里町指定史跡の保存目的調査- 概要版」]茨城大学人文社会科学部考古学研究室・城里町教育委員会（リンク（城里町ホームページ）先のWordファイル）|ref={{Harvid|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}}}。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * 報告書 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1985|chapter=|title=頓（徳）化原古墳整備調査報告書|publisher=桂村教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=城里町徳化原古墳の測量調査|title=茨城県中央部の古墳調査 -測量報告（墳丘･石室･遺物）-（茨城大学人文社会科学部考古学研究報告 第12冊）|publisher=茨城大学人文社会科学部考古学研究室|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2021|chapter=|title=頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 -茨城県城里町指定史跡の保存目的調査-|publisher=茨城大学人文社会科学部考古学研究室・城里町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=瓦吹堅先生還暦記念論文集刊行会|author=稲田健一|year=2007|chapter=東茨城郡城里町徳化原古墳について -切石石室を有する古墳の一例-|title=考古学の深層（瓦吹堅先生還暦記念論文集）|publisher=纂修堂|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=白石太一郎先生傘寿記念論文集編集委員会|author=田中裕|year=2019|chapter=考古学からみた「里長」の系譜|title=古墳と国家形成期の諸問題（白石太一郎先生傘寿記念論文集）|publisher=山川出版社|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html 頓（徳）化原古墳] - 城里町教育委員会 {{DEFAULTSORT:とつけはらこふん}} [[Category:茨城県の古墳]] [[Category:城里町の歴史]] [[Category:長方形墳]] [[Category:茨城県内の市町村指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[徳化原古墳]] to [[Tokkehara Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 徳化原古墳 / 頓化原古墳 |画像 = [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
墳丘・石室開口部 |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[茨城県]][[東茨城郡]][[城里町]]北方1478-1ほか（字徳化）
（茨城県埋蔵文化財センター内） |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 30|緯度秒 = 13.03 |経度度 =140|経度分 = 21|経度秒 = 27.05 |ISO = JP-08 |形状 = 長方形墳 |規模 = 37.8m×24m |埋葬施設 = [[横穴式石室]] |出土品 = 青銅製帯金具・[[須恵器]]・[[土師器]] |陪塚 = |築造時期 = [[7世紀]]後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = 城里町指定史跡「頓（徳）化原古墳」 |有形文化財 = |特記事項 = |地図2= {{Location map|Japan Ibaraki|lat_deg=36|lat_min=30|lat_sec=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|label=徳化原古墳|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=茨城県内の位置|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''徳化原古墳'''（とっけはらこふん）または'''頓化原古墳'''は、[[茨城県]][[東茨城郡]][[城里町]]北方（きたかた）にある[[古墳]]。形状は長方形墳。城里町指定史跡に指定されている（指定名称は「頓（徳）化原古墳」）。 == 概要 == 茨城県北部、[[那珂川]]南岸の台地上斜面において山寄せで築造された単独古墳である。現在は旧北方小学校校舎（現在は茨城県埋蔵文化財センター「いせきぴあ茨城」）北側に所在する。[[江戸時代]]以前から石室が開口したといい、近年に測量・発掘調査が実施されている。 墳形は長方形で、東西37.8メートル・南北24（22-30）メートルを測る{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}（調査前は[[前方後円墳]]とする説もあった）。墳丘は多段築（4段以上）の階段ピラミッド構造で、墳丘上には柱穴列が認められる{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。また墳丘周囲には周溝が巡らされ、周溝隅部を中心に[[須恵器]]（壺）・[[土師器]]（甕・高坏・坏）が検出されている{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。埋葬施設は[[横穴式石室]]で、南方向に開口する。凝灰質泥岩の切石によって構築された整美な石室で、玄室・羨道からなる単室構造である{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。石室内の副葬品は失われているが、調査では青銅製帯金具（鉸具）・須恵器片が検出されている{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。 築造時期は[[古墳時代]][[終末期古墳|終末期]]の[[7世紀]]後半頃と推定される{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。特に青銅製帯金具は官僚制における初期唐風官服の帯の一部とされ、[[律令制]]施行に際して地方における地域再編の実態を知るうえで重要視される古墳になる{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。 古墳域は[[1973年]]（[[昭和]]48年）に旧桂村指定史跡に指定されている（現在は城里町指定史跡）。 == 遺跡歴 == * [[1973年]]（[[昭和]]48年）1月20日、旧桂村指定史跡（現在は城里町指定史跡）に指定。 * [[1984年]]（昭和59年）、村史編纂に伴う発掘調査。その後に石室補修（桂村教育委員会、[[1985年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。 * [[2011年]]（[[平成]]23年）、[[東日本大震災]]に伴う石室損壊。その後に石室補修{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。 * [[2015年]]（平成27年）、茨城県埋蔵文化財センター「いせきぴあ茨城」の開設{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。 * [[2016年]]（平成28年）、測量調査（[[茨城大学]]考古学研究室、[[2018年]]に報告）{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。 * [[2018年|2018]]-[[2019年]]度（平成30-[[令和]]元年度）、範囲確認調査：第1・2次調査（茨城大学考古学研究室・城里町教育委員会、[[2021年]]に報告書刊行）{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。 == 埋葬施設 == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|石室 玄室}}]] 埋葬施設としては[[横穴式石室]]が構築されており、南方向に開口する。玄室・羨道からなる単室構造の石室である。石室の規模は次の通り{{Sfn|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}。 * 石室全長：現存3.58メートル * 玄室：長さ2.30メートル、幅1.70メートル、高さ1.75メートル * 羨道：長さ1.28メートル（玄門含む）、幅1.69メートル 石室の石材は凝灰質泥岩の切石で、羨道は礫床である。 == 文化財 == === 城里町指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 頓（徳）化原古墳 - 1973年（昭和48年）1月20日指定[https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html 頓（徳）化原古墳]（城里町教育委員会）。。 == 関連施設 == * 茨城県埋蔵文化財センター「いせきぴあ茨城」（城里町北方） - 徳化原古墳に隣接。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用した文献）}} * 史跡説明板（城里町教育委員会設置） * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1982|chapter=徳化古墳群|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 8 茨城県の地名|publisher=[[平凡社]]|isbn=4582490085|ref={{Harvid|徳化古墳群（平凡社）|1982}}}} * {{Wikicite|reference=[https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html 「頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 -茨城県城里町指定史跡の保存目的調査- 概要版」]茨城大学人文社会科学部考古学研究室・城里町教育委員会（リンク（城里町ホームページ）先のWordファイル）|ref={{Harvid|頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 概要版}}}}。 == 関連文献 == {{small|（記事執筆に使用していない関連文献）}} * 報告書 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1985|chapter=|title=頓（徳）化原古墳整備調査報告書|publisher=桂村教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2018|chapter=城里町徳化原古墳の測量調査|title=茨城県中央部の古墳調査 -測量報告（墳丘･石室･遺物）-（茨城大学人文社会科学部考古学研究報告 第12冊）|publisher=茨城大学人文社会科学部考古学研究室|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=2021|chapter=|title=頓（徳）化原古墳の研究 -茨城県城里町指定史跡の保存目的調査-|publisher=茨城大学人文社会科学部考古学研究室・城里町教育委員会|isbn=|ref=}} * その他 ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=瓦吹堅先生還暦記念論文集刊行会|author=稲田健一|year=2007|chapter=東茨城郡城里町徳化原古墳について -切石石室を有する古墳の一例-|title=考古学の深層（瓦吹堅先生還暦記念論文集）|publisher=纂修堂|isbn=|ref=}} ** {{Cite book|和書|editor=白石太一郎先生傘寿記念論文集編集委員会|author=田中裕|year=2019|chapter=考古学からみた「里長」の系譜|title=古墳と国家形成期の諸問題（白石太一郎先生傘寿記念論文集）|publisher=山川出版社|isbn=|ref=}} == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html 頓（徳）化原古墳] - 城里町教育委員会 {{DEFAULTSORT:とつけはらこふん}} [[Category:茨城県の古墳]] [[Category:城里町の歴史]] [[Category:長方形墳]] [[Category:茨城県内の市町村指定史跡]]

{{afc comment|1=See [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] as this is a member[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 22:44, 22 April 2025 (UTC)}} [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "burial"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|en|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |name = Tokkehara Kofun / Tonkehara Kofun |image = [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and Stone Chamber Opening |alternate_name = | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Higashiibaraki District|ja|東茨城郡|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kitakata 1478-1, others (Tokke area)
(within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center) |coordinates = 36|coordinates = 30|coordinates = 13.03 |coordinates =140|coordinates = 21|coordinates = 27.05 |omitted = JP-08 |type = Rectangular mound |area = 37.8m×24m |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |burial goods = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |陪塚 = |built = Late 7th century |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|coordinates=36|coordinates=30|coordinates=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|map_dot_label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|ja|城里町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|ja|茨城県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The mound is a rectangular Kofun and is Historic Site1 as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River|ja|那珂川|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single Kofun on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the typeer Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|ja|江戸時代|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|keyhole-shaped tumulus|ja|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where burial goods such as {{ill|Sue pottery|ja|須恵器|1=Sue pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji pottery|ja|土師器|1=Haji pottery|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue pottery have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official unitype belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō system|ja|律令制|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer Katsura Village in 1973, and this Historic Site is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|ja|東日本大震災|qq=draft|qq=draft}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == burial == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following area: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Historic Site == === Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (Historic Site1 January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{draft categories| }} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it 2]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Kofun in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Shirosato]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofuns]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tokkehara Kofun]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]][[Category:Secondary Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tokkehara Kofun]] [[ja:徳化原古墳]] [[sn:oku Kahara Kofun]] {{translated page|ja|徳化原古墳|version=99995243|comment=Imported full ja history}}

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them.
