The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official unitype belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer Katsura Village in 1973, and this Historic Site is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the [[:en:Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]]; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == burial == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following area: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Historic Site == === Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (Historic Site1 January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|徳化原古墳|version=99995243|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:徳化原古墳]] {{Commons category|Tokkehara Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q114289628}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Kofun of Ibaraki prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shirosato]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. (within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center) |coordinates = 36|coordinates = 30|coordinates = 13.03 |coordinates =140|coordinates = 21|coordinates = 27.05 |omitted = JP-08 |type = Rectangular mound |area = 37.8m×24m |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Bronze belt fittings, [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]], [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] |陪塚 = |built = Late 7th century |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|coordinates=36|coordinates=30|coordinates=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|map_dot_label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Shirosato, Ibaraki|Shirosato, Ibaraki]], Higashiibaraki District, [[:en:Ibaraki Prefecture|Ibaraki Prefecture]]. The mound is a rectangular Kofun and is Historic Site1 as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the [[:en:Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]], in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single Kofun on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the typeer Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where burial goods such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (pots) and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue pottery have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official unitype belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer Katsura Village in 1973, and this Historic Site is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the [[:en:Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]]; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == burial == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following area: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Historic Site == === Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (Historic Site1 January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|徳化原古墳|version=99995243|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:徳化原古墳]] {{Commons category|Tokkehara Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q114289628}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Kofun of Ibaraki prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shirosato]] [[Category:Qq]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The mound is a rectangular Kofun and is Historic Site1 as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the [[:en:Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|Naka River]], in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single Kofun on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the typeer Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the [[:en:Edo period|Edo period]], with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]], but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where burial goods such as [[:en:Sue pottery|Sue pottery]] (pots) and [[:en:Haji pottery|Haji pottery]] (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue pottery have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official unitype belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the [[:en:Ritsuryō|Ritsuryō system]]. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer Katsura Village in 1973, and this Historic Site is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the [[:en:Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami]]; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == burial == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following area: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Historic Site == === Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (Historic Site1 January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|徳化原古墳|version=99995243|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:徳化原古墳]] {{Commons category|Tokkehara Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q114289628}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Kofun of Ibaraki prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shirosato]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|徳化原古墳|version=99995243|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:徳化原古墳]] {{Commons category|Tokkehara Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q114289628}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Kofun of Ibaraki prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shirosato]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Rectangular Kofun|ja|長方形墳|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rectangular Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |name = Tokkehara Kofun / Tonkehara Kofun |image = [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, funkyu.jpg|280px]]
Mound and Stone Chamber Opening |alternate_name = | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|en|Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|WD=Q1154667|lt=Higashiibaraki District}}, {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|en|Shirosato, Ibaraki|WD=Q1203068|lt=Shirosato, Ibaraki}} Kitakata 1478-1, others (Tokke area)
(within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center) |coordinates = 36|coordinates = 30|coordinates = 13.03 |coordinates =140|coordinates = 21|coordinates = 27.05 |omitted = JP-08 |type = Rectangular mound |area = 37.8m×24m |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} |陪塚 = |built = Late 7th century |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|coordinates=36|coordinates=30|coordinates=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|map_dot_label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|en|Shirosato, Ibaraki|WD=Q1203068|lt=Shirosato, Ibaraki}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. The mound is a rectangular Kofun and is Historic Site1 as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|en|Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|WD=Q1076983|lt=Naka River}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single Kofun on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the typeer Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where burial goods such as {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue pottery have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official unitype belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer Katsura Village in 1973, and this Historic Site is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q1136168|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == burial == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following area: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Historic Site == === Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (Historic Site1 January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|徳化原古墳|version=99995243|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:徳化原古墳]] {{Commons category|Tokkehara Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q114289628}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Kofun of Ibaraki prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shirosato]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official unitype belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer Katsura Village in 1973, and this Historic Site is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q1136168|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == burial == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following area: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Historic Site == === Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (Historic Site1 January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|徳化原古墳|version=99995243|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:徳化原古墳]] {{Commons category|Tokkehara Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q114289628}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Kofun of Ibaraki prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shirosato]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

Mound and Stone Chamber Opening |alternate_name = | part_of = |location = {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}, {{ill|Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|en|Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki|WD=Q1154667|lt=Higashiibaraki District|1=Higashiibaraki District, Ibaraki}}, {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|en|Shirosato, Ibaraki|WD=Q1203068|lt=Shirosato, Ibaraki|1=Shirosato, Ibaraki}} Kitakata 1478-1, others (Tokke area)
(within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center) |coordinates = 36|coordinates = 30|coordinates = 13.03 |coordinates =140|coordinates = 21|coordinates = 27.05 |omitted = JP-08 |type = Rectangular mound |area = 37.8m×24m |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} |陪塚 = |built = Late 7th century |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|coordinates=36|coordinates=30|coordinates=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|map_dot_label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|en|Shirosato, Ibaraki|WD=Q1203068|lt=Shirosato, Ibaraki|1=Shirosato, Ibaraki}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. The mound is a rectangular Kofun and is Historic Site1 as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|en|Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|WD=Q1076983|lt=Naka River|1=Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single Kofun on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the typeer Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where burial goods such as {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue pottery have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official unitype belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer Katsura Village in 1973, and this Historic Site is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q1136168|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|1=Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == burial == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following area: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Historic Site == === Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (Historic Site1 January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|徳化原古墳|version=99995243|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:徳化原古墳]] {{Commons category|Tokkehara Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q114289628}} [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Kofun of Ibaraki prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shirosato]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue pottery have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official unitype belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer Katsura Village in 1973, and this Historic Site is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q1136168|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|1=Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == burial == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following area: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Historic Site == === Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (Historic Site1 January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|徳化原古墳|version=99995243|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:徳化原古墳]] {{Commons category|Tokkehara Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q114289628}} [[Category:Kofun of Ibaraki prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shirosato]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages]]

(within the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center) |coordinates = 36|coordinates = 30|coordinates = 13.03 |coordinates =140|coordinates = 21|coordinates = 27.05 |omitted = JP-08 |type = Rectangular mound |area = 37.8m×24m |Burial Facility = {{ill|Horizontal stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Horizontal stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} |burial goods = Bronze belt fittings, {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}, {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} |陪塚 = |built = Late 7th century |Interred Persons = |陵墓 = |史跡 = Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site "Tokkehara Kofun" |有形文化財 = |Special Notes = |地図2= {{location map|Japan Ibaraki|coordinates=36|coordinates=30|coordinates=13.03|lon_deg=140|lon_min=21|lon_sec=27.05|map_dot_label=Tokkehara Kofun|mark=方墳.svg|marksize=23|caption=location in Ibaraki Prefecture|position=bottom|width=200|float=center|border=none}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|36|30|13.03|N|140|21|27.05|E}}}} }} '''Tokkehara Kofun''' (徳化原古墳) or '''Tonkehara Kofun''' (頓化原古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shirosato, Ibaraki|en|Shirosato, Ibaraki|WD=Q1203068|lt=Shirosato, Ibaraki|1=Shirosato, Ibaraki}}, Higashiibaraki District, {{ill|Ibaraki Prefecture|en|Ibaraki Prefecture|WD=Q83273|lt=Ibaraki Prefecture|1=Ibaraki Prefecture}}. The mound is a rectangular Kofun and is Historic Site1 as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|en|Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|WD=Q1076983|lt=Naka River|1=Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single Kofun on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the typeer Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The mound measures 37.8 meters east-west and 24 meters north-south. It was previously believed to be a {{ill|Zenpokoenfun|en|Zenpokoenfun|WD=Q11268718|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpōkōenfun}}, but research confirmed it to be a rectangular stepped pyramid structure with more than four tiers. Post holes have been identified on the mound’s surface. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where burial goods such as {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue pottery have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. The mound is surrounded by a moat, where burial goods such as {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|WD=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}} (pots) and {{ill|Haji pottery|en|Haji pottery|WD=Q5639938|lt=Haji pottery|1=Haji pottery}} (jars, tall bowls, and dishes) have been found, particularly at the moat’s corners. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue pottery have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}. The bronze belt fittings are believed to be part of an early Tang-style official unitype belt, highlighting the kofun’s significance in understanding regional reorganization during the establishment of the {{ill|Ritsuryō|en|Ritsuryō|WD=Q742460|lt=Ritsuryō system|1=Ritsuryō}}. The site was Historic Site1 a historic site by the typeer Katsura Village in 1973, and this Historic Site is now carried by Shirosato Town. == Historical Development == * '''1973 (Shōwa 48):''' Designated as a Katsura Village Historic Site (now Shirosato Town Historic Site). * '''1984 (Shōwa 59):''' Excavation conducted as part of a village history project, followed by stone chamber restoration (reported in 1985). * '''2011 (Heisei 23):''' Stone chamber damaged in the {{ill|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|en|Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|WD=Q1136168|lt=2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami|1=Aftermath of the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami}}; subsequently repaired. * '''2015 (Heisei 27):''' Opening of the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." * '''2016 (Heisei 28):''' Survey conducted by the Ibaraki University Archaeology Laboratory, reported in 2018. * '''2018-2019 (Heisei 30–Reiwa 1):''' Range confirmation surveys conducted in two phases, with findings published in 2021. == burial == [[File:Tokkehara Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone Chamber: Main Room}}]] The horizontal stone chamber consists of a main chamber (玄室) and an entrance passage (羨道), with the following area: * '''Total Length:''' 3.58 meters (current extent) * '''Main Chamber:''' 2.30 meters long, 1.70 meters wide, 1.75 meters high * '''Entrance Passage:''' 1.28 meters long (including the entrance), 1.69 meters wide The chamber floor is paved with pebbles, and the walls are constructed from cut tuffaceous mudstone. == Historic Site == === Shirosato Town Designated Historic Site === * '''Historic Site''' ** Tokkehara Kofun (Historic Site1 January 20, 1973) == Related Facilities == * '''Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki"''' (Shirosato Town Kitakata) – Adjacent to Tokkehara Kofun. == References == {{reflist}} == Further Reading == {{small|（Additional resources not cited in the article）}} * '''Survey Reports''' ** Katsura Village Board of Education. _Tokkehara Kofun Restoration Report._ 1985. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University. _Survey of Tokkehara Kofun in Shirosato Town._ 2018. ** Archaeology Laboratory, Ibaraki University; Shirosato Town Board of Education. _Study of Tokkehara Kofun._ 2021. == External Links == {{Commonscat|Tokkehara Kofun}} * [https://www.town.shirosato.lg.jp/page/page000052.html Tokkehara Kofun - Shirosato Town Board of Education] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|徳化原古墳|version=99995243|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:徳化原古墳]] {{Commons category|Tokkehara Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q114289628}} [[Category:Kofun of Ibaraki prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Ibaraki Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shirosato]] [[Category:Rectangular Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

The mound is a rectangular Kofun and is Historic Site1 as a Shirosato Town Historic Site under the name "Tokkehara Kofun." == Overview == The kofun is situated on a terrace along the southern bank of the {{ill|Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|en|Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)|WD=Q1076983|lt=Naka River|1=Naka River (Tochigi Ibaraki)}}, in the northern part of Ibaraki Prefecture. It was constructed as a single Kofun on a slope adjacent to a hill. Currently, it is located north of the typeer Kitakata Elementary School, now home to the Ibaraki Prefecture Buried designation1 Center, "Isekipia Ibaraki." The stone chamber has reportedly been open since before the {{ill|Edo period|en|Edo period|WD=Q184963|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period}}, with recent surveys and excavations providing additional insights. The burial facility is a horizontal stone chamber opening to the south. The chamber is a single-room structure constructed with neatly cut tuffaceous mudstone blocks. While the original grave goods have been lost, bronze belt fittings and fragments of Sue pottery have been discovered during investigations. The construction period is estimated to be the late 7th century during the {{ill|terminal Kofun period|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/terminal Kofun period|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}.
